alaska.name: Alaska Area
alaska.description: The Alaska Area serves over 178,000 Alaska Native and American Indian people across 229 federally recognized tribes, covering the entire state of Alaska.

albuquerque.name: Albuquerque Area
albuquerque.description: The Albuquerque Area provides healthcare to Pueblo, Navajo, and Apache communities across New Mexico and parts of Colorado and Texas.

bemidji.name: Bemidji Area
bemidji.description: The Bemidji Area covers tribes in Minnesota, Wisconsin, Michigan, and Indiana, including the Ojibwe, Menominee, Oneida, and Potawatomi nations.

billings.name: Billings Area
billings.description: The Billings Area serves tribal nations in Montana and Wyoming, including the Crow, Northern Cheyenne, Blackfeet, and Eastern Shoshone and Northern Arapaho.

california.name: California Area
california.description: The California Area covers the largest AI/AN population of any IHS area, serving over 100 federally recognized tribes and many urban Indian communities.

great_plains.name: Great Plains Area
great_plains.description: The Great Plains Area serves Lakota, Dakota, Nakota, and other tribal nations in South Dakota, North Dakota, Nebraska, and Iowa. The area faces some of the most severe health disparities in Indian Country.

nashville.name: Nashville Area
nashville.description: The Nashville Area serves tribes across the eastern United States, from Maine to Florida and west to Texas, including the Eastern Band of Cherokee, Mississippi Choctaw, and many others.

navajo.name: Navajo Area
navajo.description: The Navajo Area serves the Navajo Nation, the largest reservation in the United States spanning Arizona, New Mexico, and Utah. Many communities are extremely remote with limited access to care.

oklahoma_city.name: Oklahoma City Area
oklahoma_city.description: The Oklahoma City Area serves the largest number of tribes of any IHS area, with over 40 tribal nations in Oklahoma, Kansas, and parts of Texas. Many facilities are tribally operated under 638 compacts.

phoenix.name: Phoenix Area
phoenix.description: The Phoenix Area serves tribal nations in Arizona, Nevada, and Utah, including the Gila River, Salt River Pima-Maricopa, Tohono O'odham, and many others. The area has the highest diabetes prevalence rate in Indian Country.

portland.name: Portland Area
portland.description: The Portland Area serves tribes across Oregon, Washington, and Idaho, including the Confederated Tribes of Warm Springs, Yakama, Tulalip, and many others.

tucson.name: Tucson Area
tucson.description: The Tucson Area serves the Tohono O'odham Nation and Pascua Yaqui Tribe in southern Arizona. The area has the highest diabetes prevalence rate nationally and significant challenges with provider access in remote desert communities.

_national.name: National Average
_national.description: U.S. national averages for comparison. AI/AN communities experience significant disparities across nearly all health measures.
